reasons: Natalia punishes Cecilion's need for safe backline positioning.
Her camouflage and silence pressure can stop Cecilion from freely cycling long-range poke.

proof.summary: Cecilion wants to cast safely from distance, while Natalia can approach through fog or bush and force a close-range pickoff before Cecilion stabilizes.
failureCases: Cecilion stays grouped behind tank vision.
Cecilion survives the first burst with defensive tools.

reasons: Harley can enter quickly, mark Cecilion with burst pressure, then return with Space Escape.
This directly punishes Cecilion's fragile mage positioning without requiring long chase time.

proof.summary: Cecilion wants to stay at long casting range, while Harley can blink in with Space Escape, apply Deadly Magic burst pressure, and return before Cecilion fully answers.
worksBestWhen: Harley attacks from side vision or fog.
Cecilion has no Flicker, Winter item, or strong peel nearby.
failureCases: Harley's ultimate is blocked or fails to isolate Cecilion.
Cecilion is protected by instant CC or tank peel.
Harley returns to a bad hat position.

reasons: Hayabusa can bypass frontline spacing and punish Cecilion when he is isolated.
His burst is strongest when Cecilion is separated from nearby allies.

proof.summary: Cecilion wants stable long-range space, while Hayabusa can use shadow mobility to reach and burst him during isolated windows.
worksBestWhen: Hayabusa enters after enemy CC is used.
failureCases: Hayabusa's ultimate damage spreads to multiple targets.

reasons: Kaja can punish Cecilion if he steps too far forward.
Suppression gives a clean lock window against a mage who depends on distance.

proof.summary: Cecilion wants to remain unreachable, while Kaja can suppress and drag him into follow-up damage if Cecilion enters range.
worksBestWhen: Allies are ready to burst Cecilion during suppression.
failureCases: Kaja uses ultimate on a lower-value target.

reasons: Chou can isolate Cecilion from safe backline terrain.
This counter depends heavily on flank angle and execution.

proof.summary: Cecilion wants protected backline spacing, while Chou can flank and displace him into a punish position.
worksBestWhen: Cecilion's Flicker or defensive tool is unavailable.
failureCases: Chou engages through visible front angles.
Chou cannot convert the kick into team damage.
